mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, with Rank IC below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0 percent, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0 percent, or the signal loses predictive power during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates broad-based demand strength that sustains corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive movement of GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60-day equity returns, with a rolling Rank IC below 0.04 or a t-statistic below 1.5 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index during a late-cycle expansion reflects supply-side bottlenecks and margin compression, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle expansions are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in over 60 percent of cases, or the predictive coefficient flips sign when the regime shifts to early-cycle recovery.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 240.0, elevated inflation expectations force markets to price in tighter financial conditions and lower real earnings, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 5-day returns in more than 70 percent of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 240.0, or the signal shows zero incremental predictive power over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening that supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The co-movement of PAYEMS increases and UNRATE decreases fails to predict positive 20-day equity returns, with a rolling Rank IC below 0.03 or a negative out-of-sample hit rate below 50 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts signal rising residential investment and broader economic confidence, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST increases are followed by negative 60-day SPX returns in more than 55 percent of cases, or the series shows no statistically significant correlation with forward equity returns over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production exceeds 105.0, it indicates strong manufacturing output and capacity utilization that boosts corporate profit margins, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO values above 105.0 are followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in over 60 percent of instances, or the predictive signal decays with a Rank IC below 0.02 during early-cycle recovery phases.
